title: Notes write/actions/history/batch packaged-runtime ownership bundle
why_b_did_not_continue_to_more_b_next_it: The Go notes bundle is a local/copied-DB candidate behind an explicit flag, not the default packaged runtime owner.
Live route ownership, Caddy/systemd rollout, production rollback, CSRF/origin review, and frontend default routing are not authorized.
Notes delete and batch delete are DB-parity covered here; destructive media file cleanup remains part of the files/uploads/cleanup blocker before no-Python packaging can be claimed.

Category/tag live create/delete/merge, uploads/attachments, import/export, prompt/wizard options, migrations/schema/db maintenance, frontend static default, and rollback owner remain retained-Python responsibilities under the final B closure instead of becoming automatic B-next work.

response_cases: Create note with category, remarks, tags, source URLs, and pinned flag
Update note with content history insertion, category, tags, source URLs, and archive flag
state_cases: Notes rows match Python after the full bundle flow, excluding timestamp-only drift
Tags and Note_Tags match Python after create/update/batch/duplicate
Note_History content and diff summaries match Python after update/restore/delete-history
Failure and disabled-default paths leave copied DB state unchanged
parity_contract.known_local_candidate_limit: Image file cleanup parity is not promoted by this bundle; media cleanup stays under the files/uploads/cleanup blocker. Notes delete and batch delete intentionally match current Python DB behavior instead of adding new child-row cleanup semantics.

notes_write_coverage: Explicit notes-write artifact starts against copied DB
PUT /api/notes/<id> creates history on copied DB

not_authorized_by_b_next_1: Uploads, attachments, cleanup, import/export, prompt/wizard, migration, system, server, or frontend static ownership
Start C. Go packaged runtime release candidate
Start D. Live cutover and rollback proof
remaining_no_python_gaps_closed_as_retai: Promote or explicitly reject live/default ownership for the notes write bundle
Close media cleanup side effects for notes delete/batch delete
Frontend static/default owner and rollback owner

executed_followup_decision.title: Notes bundle live/default ownership decision and media-cleanup boundary
executed_followup_decision.decision: Do not promote the B-next.1 notes bundle to live/default owner yet.
executed_followup_decision.reason: B-next.1 is copied-DB only and notes delete/batch delete still have Python-owned media cleanup side effects.
executed_followup_decision.simple_explanation: Go can edit notes in a test DB, but live deletion also has to safely delete or preserve uploaded image files.
executed_followup_decision.final_b_role: historical decision only; B final closure stops the automatic B-next chain
